Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALCAL SPECIALTY CONSTRACTING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is denied as moot as to plaintiff. Plaintiff dismissed its complaint against Alcal Specialty Contracting Inc. on November 2, 2022. Denied as moot as to cross-complaintant Fisher Development, Inc. as Fisher dismissed its cross-complaint as to Alcal Specialty Contracting Inc. on October 24, 2022. C/S Erectors, Inc. lacked standing to oppose this motion since it was not directed towards a C/S Erector pleading. "It is well settled in California ... that the exoneration of a joint tortfeasor from liability does not 'aggrieve' the other individually liable tortfeasor(s) insofar as that word is understood to apply to a party's standing to appeal." (Holt v. Booth (1991) 1 Cal.App.4th 1074, 1080; see also Swails v. General Electric Co. (1968) 264 Cal.App.2d 82, 86 ["It is established law that a defendant who is himself liable is not aggrieved by the exoneration of a codefendant. [Citations.] This rule was not changed by the enactment of [the statutory scheme] relating to contribution among joint tortfeasors."]); Diamond Springs Lime Co. v. American River Constructors (1971) 16 Cal.App.3d 581, 608 ["[A] defendant having independent liability has no standing to appeal from a judgment exonerating his codefendant, even where a potential right of contribution exists."]; Cook v. Superior Court (1969) 274 Cal.App.2d 675, 679 ["A defendant who is individually liable is not aggrieved by the exoneration, even though erroneous, of a codefendant."].)

In any event, C/S Erector did not file a timely supplemental opposition that creates a triable issue of fact and the motion is now moot.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Continued To Nov-10-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT PIERING, Esq. of Piering Law Firm (Tel: 916-446-1944) for plaintiff;
Atty. KATHERINE SANDOVAL, Esq. of Matthiesen Wickert (Tel: 800-637-9176) for intervenor Travelers Property Casualty;
Atty. GUSTAVO PENA, Esq. of Harris & Hermanson (Tel: 415-777-1308) for defendant Fisher Development;
Attys. KIME SMITH, Esq. and CARLOS POZA, Esq. of Manning Gross (Tel: 925-947-1300) for defendant Alcal Speciality Contracting;
Atty. LOUIS BARELLA, Esq. of Tyson & Mendes (Tel: 628-253-5070) for cross-defendant CS Erectors.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALCAL SPECIALTY CONSTRACTING, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Cross-defendant Alcal Specialty Contracting, Inc.'s ("Alcal") motion for summary judgment is continued to November 10, 2022. The supplemental opposition is due on October 27, 2022. The supplemental reply is due on November 4, 2022.

"To demonstrate good faith, the party seeking a continuance must submit an affidavit or declaration showing that (1) the facts to be obtained are essential to opposing the motion; (2) there is reason to believe such facts may exist; and (3) the reasons why additional time is needed to obtain these facts." (Jade Fashion & Co. v. Harkham Indus., Inc. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 635, 656 (internal quotations omitted).) A continuance is "virtually mandated upon a good faith showing by affidavit that a continuance is needed to obtain facts essential to justify opposition to the motion." (Bahl v. Bank of America (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 389, 395 (internal quotations omitted).)

Cross-complainant Fisher Development, Inc. ("Fisher") submits a declaration showing that the facts to be obtained from Alcal's person most knowledgeable are essential to opposing the motion. (See Klein Decl.) Specifically, Fisher seeks information from Alcal regarding its employees' and/or contractors' presence and work on the roof where the subject accident took place in the period of time before the accident in question. Alcal's motion turns on these essential facts. (See Mot., 6:1-2 ["Alcal did not create the hole that caused Plaintiff's fall [and its employees were not] on the jobsite on August 13, 2018 at the time of the accident."].) Fisher shows that there is reason to believe Alcal's person most knowledgeable has the aforementioned facts. Finally, Fisher shows that additional time is needed because it has not had the opportunity to depose Alcal's person most knowledgeable. Fisher filed the cross-complaint just five months ago, on April 7, 2022.

Co-cross-defendant C/S Erectors also filed an "opposition" to Alcal's motion for summary judgment in which it seeks a continuance for similar reasons. C/S Erectors does not have standing to oppose a co-defendant's motion. (See CCP 437c(p)(2) [once a defendant moving for summary judgment meets their initial burden, "the burden shifts to the plaintiff or cross-complainant" to show triable issues of fact] (emphasis added).] C/S Erectors is neither a plaintiff nor cross-complainant. As such, C/S Erectors does not have standing to request a continuance.

In any case, cross-complainant Fisher has made a good faith showing and the motion for summary judgment is continued to November 10, 2022.

Counsel for Fisher Development to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
